45	                   e_pic_type pic_on_screen_val,
46	                   std::shared_ptr<const SetupTimingInfo> timing_info);
47	
48	/**
49	 * @brief Mark a stage as fully complete.
50	 *
51	 * Records that the given stage (PLACEMENT, ROUTING, ANALYTICAL_PLACEMENT, …)
52	 * has finished. The `wait_for_stage <stage>_done` graphics-command barrier
53	 * advances only when both the current pic_on_screen matches the requested
54	 * stage AND that stage has been marked complete here. This lets scripted
55	 * commands defer past per-iteration update_screen() checkpoints and run
56	 * only on the post-stage update_screen() where the underlying contexts
57	 * (route_ctx, place_ctx, …) are fully settled.
58	 *
59	 * Call once per stage immediately before that stage's final update_screen.
60	 */
61	void notify_stage_complete(e_pic_type stage);
62	
63	//FIXME: Currently broken if no rr-graph is loaded
64	/**
65	 * @brief Load the arrays containing the left and bottom coordinates of the clbs.
66	 * forming the FPGA.
67	 *
68	 * @param clb_width The width and height of a drawn clb.
69	 * @param blk_loc_registry A reference to placement location information.
70	 */
71	void init_draw_coords(float clb_width, const BlkLocRegistry& blk_loc_registry);
72	
73	/**
74	 * @brief Set the intial_world ezgl::rectangle for analytical placement
75	 * 
76	 * This function sets graphic initial dimensions so there are no gaps between blocks
77	 */
78	void set_initial_world_ap();
79	
80	/**
81	 * @brief Set the intial_world ezgl::rectangle for default
82	 *
83	 * This function sets graphic initial dimensions so there are gaps between blocks
84	 */
85	void set_initial_world();
86	
87	/* Sets the static show_graphics and gr_automode variables to the    *
88	 * desired values.  They control if graphics are enabled and, if so, *
89	 * how often the user is prompted for input.                         */
90	void init_graphics_state(bool show_graphics_val,
91	                         int gr_automode_val,
92	                         enum e_route_type route_type,
93	                         bool save_graphics,
94	                         std::string graphics_commands,
95	                         std::string renderer_type,
96	                         bool is_flat);
97	
98	/* Allocates the structures needed to draw the placement and routing.*/
99	void alloc_draw_structs(const t_arch* arch);
100	
101	/* Free everything allocated by alloc_draw_structs. Called after close_graphics()
102	 * in vpr_api.c. */
103	void free_draw_structs();
104	
105	#ifndef NO_GRAPHICS
106	const ezgl::color SELECTED_COLOR = ezgl::GREEN;
107	const ezgl::color DRIVES_IT_COLOR = ezgl::RED;
108	const ezgl::color DRIVEN_BY_IT_COLOR = ezgl::LIGHT_MEDIUM_BLUE;
109	
110	const float WIRE_DRAWING_WIDTH = 0.5;
111	
112	/**
113	 * @brief Calculate the ratio between pixels and world units spanning the screen width.
114	 * 
115	 * @return Returns the ratio described above, which is usually used to determine when decluttering should occur.
116	 */
117	double get_pixels_per_world_unit(ezgl::renderer* g);
118	
119	/* Find the edge between two rr nodes */
120	t_edge_size find_edge(RRNodeId prev_inode, RRNodeId inode);
121	
122	/* Returns the track number of this routing resource node inode. */
123	int get_track_num(int inode, const vtr::OffsetMatrix<int>& chanx_track, const vtr::OffsetMatrix<int>& chany_track);
124	
125	//Returns the drawing coordinates of the specified pin
126	ezgl::point2d atom_pin_draw_coord(AtomPinId pin);
127	
128	//Returns the drawing coordinates of the specified tnode
129	ezgl::point2d tnode_draw_coord(tatum::NodeId node);
130	
131	/* Converts a vtr Color to a ezgl Color. */
132	ezgl::color to_ezgl_color(vtr::Color<float> color);
133	
134	/* This helper function determines whether a net has been highlighted. The highlighting
135	 * could be caused by the user clicking on a routing resource, toggled, or
136	 * fan-in/fan-out of a highlighted node. */
137	bool draw_if_net_highlighted(ParentNetId inet);
138	std::vector<RRNodeId> trace_routed_connection_rr_nodes(
139	    ParentNetId net_id,
140	    int driver_pin,
141	    int sink_pin);
142	
143	/* Helper function for trace_routed_connection_rr_nodes
144	 * Adds the rr nodes linking rt_node to sink_rr_node to rr_nodes_on_path
145	 * Returns true if rt_node is on the path. */
146	bool trace_routed_connection_rr_nodes_recurr(const RouteTreeNode& rt_node,
147	                                             RRNodeId sink_rr_node,
148	                                             std::vector<RRNodeId>& rr_nodes_on_path);
149	
150	/* This routine highlights the blocks affected in the latest move      *
151	 * It highlights the old and new locations of the moved blocks         *
152	 * It also highlights the moved block input and output terminals       *
153	 * Currently, it is used in placer debugger when breakpoint is reached */
154	void highlight_moved_block_and_its_terminals(const t_pl_blocks_to_be_moved&);
155	
156	// pass in an (x,y,subtile) location and the color in which it should be drawn.
157	// This overrides the color of any block placed in that location, and also applies if the location is empty.
158	void set_draw_loc_color(t_pl_loc, ezgl::color);
159	
160	// clear the colored_locations vector
161	void clear_colored_locations();
162	
163	/**
164	 * @brief If the input loc is marked in colored_locations vector, the function will return true and the corresponding color is sent back in loc_color
165	 * otherwise, the function returns false (the location isn't among the highlighted locations)
166	 *
167	 * @param curr_loc  The current location that is being checked for whether it must be highlighted or not
168	 * @param loc_color The corresponding color that is to be used to highlight the block
169	 *
170	 * @return    Returns true or false depending on whether the block at the specified (x,y,layer) location needs to be highlighted by a specific color.
171	 *            The corresponding color is returned by reference.
172	 */
173	bool highlight_loc_with_specific_color(t_pl_loc curr_loc, ezgl::color& loc_color);
174	
175	/* Because the list of possible block type colours is finite, we wrap around possible colours if there are more
176	 * block types than colour choices. This ensures we support any number of types, although the colours may repeat.*/
177	ezgl::color get_block_type_color(t_physical_tile_type_ptr type);
178	
179	/* Lightens a color's luminance [0, 1] by an absolute 'amount' */
180	ezgl::color lighten_color(ezgl::color color, float amount);
181	
182	void toggle_window_mode(QWidget* /*widget*/, ezgl::application* /*app*/);
183	
184	size_t get_max_fanout();
185	
186	/**
187	 * @brief Takes in two colors and compares rgb values, ignoring transparency/alpha
188	 * Sets both transparencies to opaque and then compares the colors.
189	 */
190	bool rgb_is_same(ezgl::color color1, ezgl::color color2);
191	
192	/**
193	 * @brief Takes in the layer number of the src and sink of an element(flyline, rr_node connections, etc...) and returns a t_draw_layer_display object holding the
194	 *        information of the visibility of the element as well as the transparency based on the setting set by the user from the view menu in the UI.
195	 *
196	 * @param src_layer
197	 * @param sink_layer
198	 * @return  Returns whether the element should be drawn (true or false) and the transparency factor (0 - transparent ,255 - opaque) as a t_draw_layer_display object
199	 */
200	t_draw_layer_display get_element_visibility_and_transparency(int src_layer, int sink_layer);
201	
202	/**
203	 * @brief takes in the x and y world coordinates of where the user clicked on the screen and returns the corresponding clusterBlockId that represents
204	 *         the clb clicked upon by the user on a currently visible FPGA layer. Search for the clb begins from the top layer to ensure it
205	 *         returns the clusterBlockId of a clb on a higher layer during instances of overlap between clb blocks.
206	 * @param x
207	 * @param y
208	 * @return returns the ClusterBlockId of the clb at the specified (x,y) location (in world coordinates) as seen by looking downwards from the top of a 3D FPGA.
209	 *         Chooses the clb on the top visible layer if there are overlapping blocks. Returns INVALID(-1) otherwise,if clb is not found on any visible layer.
210	 */
211	ClusterBlockId get_cluster_block_id_from_xy_loc(double x, double y);
212	
213	#endif /* NO_GRAPHICS */
214

1	.. _vpr_graphics:
2	
3	Graphics
4	========
5	VPR includes easy-to-use graphics for visualizing both the targeted FPGA architecture, and the circuit VPR has implemented on the architecture.
6	
7	.. image:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/des90_routing_util.gif
8	    :align: center
9	
10	Enabling Graphics
11	-----------------
12	
13	Compiling with Graphics Support
14	~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
15	VPR's graphics are built on `Qt6 <https://www.qt.io/>`_. A plain ``make`` builds
16	the GUI **only if a suitable Qt6 (>= 6.9.3) is found**; otherwise VPR is built
17	headless. The 6.9.3 floor exists because earlier Qt6 releases have QRhi
18	(Render Hardware Interface, GPU accelerated) rendering bugs.
19	
20	The Qt6 GUI links against your system's OpenGL / EGL / xkbcommon runtime
21	libraries. On Debian/Ubuntu these prerequisites are installed for you by
22	``./install_apt_packages.sh``, or manually with::
23	
24	    > sudo apt-get install libxkbcommon-dev libgl-dev libegl-dev libopengl0 libegl-mesa0 libgl1-mesa-dri
25	
26	With those in place, install Qt6 itself. On the currently supported
27	distributions (e.g. Ubuntu 24.04) the system Qt6 is too old (24.04 ships
28	Qt 6.4.2), so the recommended way to get a suitable Qt6 is the repo-local SDK
29	installed by ``ensure_qt6_sdk.sh`` (no root required)::
30	
31	    > ./dev/ensure_qt6_sdk.sh
32	
33	During configuration the build reports whether graphics were enabled. If a
34	suitable Qt6 is found::
35	
36	    -- VPR Graphics: Enabled (Qt6 6.9.3 found)
37	
38	Otherwise (and VPR is built without graphics)::
39	
40	    -- VPR Graphics: Disabled (no Qt6 >= 6.9.3; run ./dev/ensure_qt6_sdk.sh or install a system Qt6 if its version >= 6.9.3)
41	
42	.. note:: On future distributions that ship Qt6 >= 6.9.3 (Ubuntu 26.04 onward)
43	    you can instead install Qt6 system-wide and skip ``ensure_qt6_sdk.sh``
44	    entirely — at which point the script becomes obsolete. The GUI needs the
45	    base, private (QRhi/QShader headers), SVG, and shader-tools (the ``qsb``
46	    shader baker) packages::
47	
48	        > sudo apt-get install qt6-base-dev qt6-base-private-dev qt6-svg-dev qt6-shadertools-dev
49	
50	Two convenience targets make the choice explicit::
51	
52	    > make ensure-gui        # build vpr WITH the GUI (runs ensure_qt6_sdk.sh first)
53	    > make ensure-headless   # build vpr WITHOUT the GUI
54	
55	.. seealso:: :doc:`Building VTR </BUILDING>` for the full build instructions.
56	
57	Enabling Graphics at Run-time
58	~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
59	When running VPR provide :option:`vpr --disp` ``on`` to enable graphics.
60	
61	Saving Graphics at Run-time
62	~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
63	When running VPR provide :option:`vpr --save_graphics` ``on`` to save an image of the final placement and the final routing created by vpr to pdf files on disk. The files are named vpr_placement.pdf and vpr_routing.pdf.
64	
65	A graphical window will now pop up when you run VPR.
66	
67	Navigation
68	----------
69	* Click on the **Zoom Fit** button to get an over-encompassing view of the FPGA architecture.
70	* Click and drag with the left mouse button to pan the view, or scroll the mouse wheel to zoom in and out.
71	* Click on the **Zoom Select** button, then on the diagonally opposite corners of a box, to zoom in on a particular area.
72	* Click on **Save** under the **Misc.** tab to save the image on screen to PDF, PNG, or SVG file.
73	* **Next Step** tells VPR to continue with the next step in placing and routing the circuit.
74	
75	
76	.. note:: Menu buttons will be greyed out when they are not selectable (e.g. VPR is working).
77	
78	Placement
79	--------------------------------
80	By default VPR's graphics displays the FPGA floorplan (block grid) and current placement.
81	
82	.. figure:: ../Images/Overall_view.png
83	    :align: center 
84	    :width: 50%
85	
86	    FPGA floorplan (block grid)    
87	
88	If the **Placement Macros** drop down is set, any placement macros (e.g. carry chains, which require specific relative placements between some blocks) will be highlighted.
89	
90	.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/neuron_placement_macros.gif
91	    :align: center
92	    :width: 50%
93	
94	    Placement with macros (carry chains) highlighted
95	
96	Nets
97	----------------
98	To visualize nets, first enable the **Display Nets** switch under the **Net** Tab.
99	
100	The user can choose between drawing nets as **Flylines** (direct connections between sources and sinks) or as **Routing** (the actual routed path of the net).
101	Only the **Flylines** option is available during placement, as routing has not yet been performed.
102	
103	The Inter-Cluster Nets and Intra-Cluster Nets options allow the user to choose whether to visualize nets between clbs or within a clb, respectively. The Intra-Cluster Routed Nets option is currently only available when **flat routing is enabled**.
104	
105	.. figure:: ../Images/Net_Settings.png
106	    :align: center 
107	    :width: 25%
108	
109	    Net Tab
110	
111	If routing is shown, clicking on a pin or channel wire will highlight the whole net in magenta.
112	Multiple nets can be highlighted by pressing ctrl + mouse click.
113	
114	.. figure:: ../Images/show_nets.gif
115	    :align: center
116	    :width: 100%
117	
118	    Visualizing Nets
119	
120	When the **Highlight Block Fan-in and Fan-out** option is enabled, clicking on an internal block will draw its fan-in, fan-out, and internal flylines in blue, red, and yellow, respectively. 
121	
122	.. figure:: ../Images/highlight_flylines.png
123	    :align: center
124	    :width: 50%
125	
126	    Highlight Block Fan-in and Fan-out Flylines
127	
128	Clicking on a clb (not the internal physical blocks) will also highlight all the fan-in and fan-out routed nets in blue and red, respectively.
129	
130	Critical Path
131	-----------------------------
132	During placement and routing you can click on the **Display Critical Path** switch under the **Net** tab to visualize the critical path.
133	Each stage between primitive pins is shown in a different colour.
134	
135	.. figure:: ../Images/crit_path.png
136	    :align: center 
137	    :width: 100%
138	
139	    Critical Path with all options enabled.
140	
141	* During placement the critical path is shown only as flylines.
142	* During routing the critical path can be shown as both flylines and routed net connections.
143	
144	.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/des90_cpd.gif
145	    :align: center
146	    :width: 50%
147	
148	    Critical Path flylines during placement and routing
149	
150	Routing Architecture
151	--------------------------------
152	
153	During the route stage, the **Route** tab provides various options to visualize router resources and statistics.
154	
155	.. figure:: ../Images/Routing_Options.png
156	    :align: center 
157	    :width: 25%
158	
159	    Routing Options
160	
161	To visualize routing architecture, first enable the **Display Routing Resources** switch under the Route tab. Then, click on the checkboxes below to show/hide the types of nodes and edges you want to visualize. 
162	
163	The intra-cluster options are currently only available when **flat routing is enabled**. 
164	
165	The **Highlight Fan-In Fan-Out Edges** option will highlight the fan-in and fan-out edges of the selected routing resource in blue and red, respectively. 
166	
167	Multiple routing resources can be highlighted by pressing ctrl + mouse click.
168	
169	.. figure:: ../Images/show_rr_graph.gif
170	    :align: center
171	    :width: 100%
172	
173	    Visualizing Routing Architecture
174	
175	**Node Colors**:
176	
177	+------------+--------+
178	| Node Type  | Color  |
179	+============+========+
180	| Channel    | Black  |
181	+------------+--------+
182	| Input Pin  | Purple |
183	+------------+--------+
184	| Output Pin | Pink   |
185	+------------+--------+
186	
187	**Edge Colors**:
188	
189	+-----------------------+---------------+
190	| Edge Type             | Color         |
191	+=======================+===============+
192	| Pin to Output Pin     | Light Pink    |
193	+-----------------------+---------------+
194	| Pin to Input Pin      | Medium Purple |
195	+-----------------------+---------------+
196	| Output Pin to Channel | Pink          |
197	+-----------------------+---------------+
198	| Channel to Input Pin  | Purple        |
199	+-----------------------+---------------+
200	| Channel to Channel    | Dark Green    |
201	+-----------------------+---------------+
202	| Non-Configurable Edge | Dark Grey     |
203	+-----------------------+---------------+
204	
205	The points at which wiring segments connect to clb pins (connection box switches) are marked with an ``x``.
206	
207	Switch box connections will have buffers (triangles) or pass transistors (circles) drawn on top of them, depending on the type of switch each connection uses.
208	
209	Routing Congestion
210	------------------------------
211	When a routing is shown on-screen, clicking on the **Congestion** drop-down menu under the **Routing Options** tab will show a heat map of any overused routing resources (wires or pins).
212	Lighter colours (e.g. yellow) correspond to highly overused resources, while darker colours (e.g. blue) correspond to lower overuse.
213	The overuse range shown at the bottom of the window.
214	
215	.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/bitcoin_congestion.gif
216	    :align: center
217	    :width: 50%
218	
219	    Routing Congestion during placement and routing
220	
221	Routing Utilization
222	-------------------------------
223	When a routing is shown on-screen, clicking on the **Routing Util** drop-down menu will show a heat map of routing wire utilization (i.e. fraction of wires used in each channel).
224	Lighter colours (e.g. yellow) correspond to highly utilized channels, while darker colours (e.g. blue) correspond to lower utilization.
225	
226	.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/bitcoin_routing_util.gif
227	    :align: center
228	    :width: 50%
229	
230	    Routing Utilization during placement and routing
231	
232	Block Internals
233	-------------------------------
234	During placement and routing you can adjust the level of block detail you visualize by using the **Block Internal** option under the **Block** tab. 
235	
236	.. figure:: ../Images/Block_Settings.png
237	    :align: center 
238	    :width: 100%
239	
240	    Block Settings 
241	
242	Each block can contain a number of flip flops (ff), look up tables (lut), and other primitives. The higher the number, the deeper into the hierarchy within the cluster level block you see. 
243	The format of the internal block names is <block_type_name>[<placement_index>]:<mode_name>.
244	
245	Layers Dropdown
246	-----------------------------
247	.. figure:: ../Images/view_menu.png
248	    :align: center 
249	    :width: 50%
250	
251	    Items under the layers dropdown
252	
253	The layers dropdown is displayed when vpr is targeting a stacked multi-die architecture (more than 1 layer).
254	Layers are drawn in ascending order for many drawing features (e.g. blocks); that is layer 0 is drawn first, and (if visible), layer 1 is drawn on top of it etc.
255	The visibility and transparency of a layer can be changed, which will affect blocks, nets, routing, and critical path.
256	Cross-layer connections refer to connections that are in different layers. 
257	
258	Button Description Table
259	------------------------
260	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
261	|      Buttons      |      Stages       |        Functionalities       |     Detailed Descriptions    |
262	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
263	| Blk Internal      | Placement/Routing | Controls depth of sub-blocks | Click multiple times to show |
264	|                   |                   | shown                        | more details; Click to reset |
265	|                   |                   |                              | when reached maximum level   |
266	|                   |                   |                              | of detail                    |
267	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
268	| Toggle Block      | Placement/Routing | Adjusts the level of         | Click multiple times to      |
269	| Internal          |                   | visualized block detail      | go deeper into the           |
270	|                   |                   |                              | hierarchy within the cluster |
271	|                   |                   |                              | level block                  |
272	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
273	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
274	| Blk Pin Util      | Placement/Routing | Visualizes block pin         | Click multiple times to      |
275	|                   |                   | utilization                  | visualize all block pin      |
276	|                   |                   |                              | utilization, input block pin |
277	|                   |                   |                              | utilization, or output block |
278	|                   |                   |                              | pin utilization              |
279	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
280	| Cong. Cost        | Routing           | Visualizes the congestion    |                              |
281	|                   |                   | costs of routing resources   |                              |
282	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
283	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
284	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
285	| Congestion        | Routing           | Visualizes a heat map of     |                              |
286	|                   |                   | overused routing resources   |                              |
287	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
288	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
289	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
290	| Crit. Path        | Placement/Routing | Visualizes the critical path |                              |
291	|                   |                   | of the circuit               |                              |
292	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
293	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
294	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
295	| Draw Partitions   | Placement/Routing | Visualizes placement         |                              |
296	|                   |                   | constraints                  |                              |
297	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
298	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
299	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
300	| Place Macros      | Placement/Routing | Visualizes placement macros  |                              |
301	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
302	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
303	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
304	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
305	| Route BB          | Routing           | Visualizes net bounding      | Click multiple times to      |
306	|                   |                   | boxes one by one             | sequence through the net     |
307	|                   |                   |                              | being shown                  |
308	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
309	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
310	| Router Cost       | Routing           | Visualizes the router costs  |                              |
311	|                   |                   | of different routing         |                              |
312	|                   |                   | resources                    |                              |
313	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
314	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
315	| Routing Util      | Routing           | Visualizes routing channel   |                              |
316	|                   |                   | utilization with colors      |                              |
317	|                   |                   | indicating the fraction of   |                              |
318	|                   |                   | wires used within a channel  |                              |
319	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
320	| Toggle Nets       | Placement/Routing | Visualizes the nets in the   | Click multiple times to      |
321	|                   |                   | circuit                      | set the nets to be visible / |
322	|                   |                   |                              | invisible                    |
323	|                   |                   |                              |                              |
324	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
325	| Toggle RR         | Placement/Routing | Visualizes different views   | Click multiple times to      |
326	|                   |                   | of the routing resources     | switch between routing       |
327	|                   |                   |                              | resources available in the   |
328	|                   |                   |                              | FPGA                         |
329	+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
330	
331	Manual Moves
332	------------
333	
334	The manual moves feature allows the user to specify the next move in placement. If the move is legal, blocks are swapped and the new move is shown on the architecture. 
335	
336	.. _fig-misc-tab:
337	.. figure:: ../Images/manual_move.png
338	    :align: center
339	    :width: 25%
340	
341	    Misc. Tab
342	
343	To enable the feature, activate the **Manual Move** toggle button under the **Misc.** tab and press Done. Alternatively, the user can activate the **Manual Move** toggle button and click on the block to be moved.
344	
345	.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/draw_manual_moves_window.png
346	   :align: center
347	   :width: 50%
348	
349	On the manual move window, the user can specify the Block ID/Block name of the block to move and the To location, with the x position, y position and subtile position. For the manual move to be valid:
350	
351	- The To location requested by the user should be within the grid's dimensions.
352	- The block to be moved is found, valid and not fixed.
353	- The blocks to be swapped are compatible.
354	- The location chosen by the user is different from the block's current location.
355	  
356	If the manual move is legal, the cost summary window will display the delta cost, delta timing, delta bounding box cost and the placer's annealing decision that would result from this move. 
357	
358	.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/manual_move_cost_dialog.png
359	   :align: center
360	   :width: 50%
361	
362	The user can Accept or Reject the manual move based on the values provided. If accepted the block's new location is shown. 
363	
364	Pause Button
365	------------
366	
367	The pause button allows the user to temporarily stop the program during placement or routing.
368	When clicked during the placement stage, the program will pause at the next temperature update.
369	When clicked during the routing stage, it will pause at the next router iteration.
370	
371	The button can be pressed at any time while the program is running. To enable the feature, click the **Pause** button under the **Misc.** tab (see :ref:`fig-misc-tab`).
372	Once the program reaches the next temperature update or router iteration after the button is pressed, it will automatically pause.
373	
374	After the program has paused, clicking **Next Step** allows the user to resume execution from the point where the program was paused.
375	This can be continuing from the current temperature in placement or from the current router iteration in routing.
376

.. _vpr_graphics:

Graphics
========
VPR includes easy-to-use graphics for visualizing both the targeted FPGA architecture, and the circuit VPR has implemented on the architecture.

.. image:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/des90_routing_util.gif
    :align: center

Enabling Graphics
-----------------

Compiling with Graphics Support
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
VPR's graphics are built on `Qt6 <https://www.qt.io/>`_. A plain ``make`` builds
the GUI **only if a suitable Qt6 (>= 6.9.3) is found**; otherwise VPR is built
headless. The 6.9.3 floor exists because earlier Qt6 releases have QRhi
(Render Hardware Interface, GPU accelerated) rendering bugs.

The Qt6 GUI links against your system's OpenGL / EGL / xkbcommon runtime
libraries. On Debian/Ubuntu these prerequisites are installed for you by
``./install_apt_packages.sh``, or manually with::

    > sudo apt-get install libxkbcommon-dev libgl-dev libegl-dev libopengl0 libegl-mesa0 libgl1-mesa-dri

With those in place, install Qt6 itself. On the currently supported
distributions (e.g. Ubuntu 24.04) the system Qt6 is too old (24.04 ships
Qt 6.4.2), so the recommended way to get a suitable Qt6 is the repo-local SDK
installed by ``ensure_qt6_sdk.sh`` (no root required)::

    > ./dev/ensure_qt6_sdk.sh

During configuration the build reports whether graphics were enabled. If a
suitable Qt6 is found::

    -- VPR Graphics: Enabled (Qt6 6.9.3 found)

Otherwise (and VPR is built without graphics)::

    -- VPR Graphics: Disabled (no Qt6 >= 6.9.3; run ./dev/ensure_qt6_sdk.sh or install a system Qt6 if its version >= 6.9.3)

.. note:: On future distributions that ship Qt6 >= 6.9.3 (Ubuntu 26.04 onward)
    you can instead install Qt6 system-wide and skip ``ensure_qt6_sdk.sh``
    entirely — at which point the script becomes obsolete. The GUI needs the
    base, private (QRhi/QShader headers), SVG, and shader-tools (the ``qsb``
    shader baker) packages::

        > sudo apt-get install qt6-base-dev qt6-base-private-dev qt6-svg-dev qt6-shadertools-dev

Two convenience targets make the choice explicit::

    > make ensure-gui        # build vpr WITH the GUI (runs ensure_qt6_sdk.sh first)
    > make ensure-headless   # build vpr WITHOUT the GUI

.. seealso:: :doc:`Building VTR </BUILDING>` for the full build instructions.

Enabling Graphics at Run-time
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
When running VPR provide :option:`vpr --disp` ``on`` to enable graphics.

Saving Graphics at Run-time
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
When running VPR provide :option:`vpr --save_graphics` ``on`` to save an image of the final placement and the final routing created by vpr to pdf files on disk. The files are named vpr_placement.pdf and vpr_routing.pdf.

A graphical window will now pop up when you run VPR.

Navigation
----------
* Click on the **Zoom Fit** button to get an over-encompassing view of the FPGA architecture.
* Click and drag with the left mouse button to pan the view, or scroll the mouse wheel to zoom in and out.
* Click on the **Zoom Select** button, then on the diagonally opposite corners of a box, to zoom in on a particular area.
* Click on **Save** under the **Misc.** tab to save the image on screen to PDF, PNG, or SVG file.
* **Next Step** tells VPR to continue with the next step in placing and routing the circuit.


.. note:: Menu buttons will be greyed out when they are not selectable (e.g. VPR is working).

Placement
--------------------------------
By default VPR's graphics displays the FPGA floorplan (block grid) and current placement.

.. figure:: ../Images/Overall_view.png
    :align: center 
    :width: 50%

    FPGA floorplan (block grid)    

If the **Placement Macros** drop down is set, any placement macros (e.g. carry chains, which require specific relative placements between some blocks) will be highlighted.

.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/neuron_placement_macros.gif
    :align: center
    :width: 50%

    Placement with macros (carry chains) highlighted

Nets
----------------
To visualize nets, first enable the **Display Nets** switch under the **Net** Tab.

The user can choose between drawing nets as **Flylines** (direct connections between sources and sinks) or as **Routing** (the actual routed path of the net).
Only the **Flylines** option is available during placement, as routing has not yet been performed.

The Inter-Cluster Nets and Intra-Cluster Nets options allow the user to choose whether to visualize nets between clbs or within a clb, respectively. The Intra-Cluster Routed Nets option is currently only available when **flat routing is enabled**.

.. figure:: ../Images/Net_Settings.png
    :align: center 
    :width: 25%

    Net Tab

If routing is shown, clicking on a pin or channel wire will highlight the whole net in magenta.
Multiple nets can be highlighted by pressing ctrl + mouse click.

.. figure:: ../Images/show_nets.gif
    :align: center
    :width: 100%

    Visualizing Nets

When the **Highlight Block Fan-in and Fan-out** option is enabled, clicking on an internal block will draw its fan-in, fan-out, and internal flylines in blue, red, and yellow, respectively. 

.. figure:: ../Images/highlight_flylines.png
    :align: center
    :width: 50%

    Highlight Block Fan-in and Fan-out Flylines

Clicking on a clb (not the internal physical blocks) will also highlight all the fan-in and fan-out routed nets in blue and red, respectively.

Critical Path
-----------------------------
During placement and routing you can click on the **Display Critical Path** switch under the **Net** tab to visualize the critical path.
Each stage between primitive pins is shown in a different colour.

.. figure:: ../Images/crit_path.png
    :align: center 
    :width: 100%

    Critical Path with all options enabled.

* During placement the critical path is shown only as flylines.
* During routing the critical path can be shown as both flylines and routed net connections.

.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/des90_cpd.gif
    :align: center
    :width: 50%

    Critical Path flylines during placement and routing

Routing Architecture
--------------------------------

During the route stage, the **Route** tab provides various options to visualize router resources and statistics.

.. figure:: ../Images/Routing_Options.png
    :align: center 
    :width: 25%

    Routing Options

To visualize routing architecture, first enable the **Display Routing Resources** switch under the Route tab. Then, click on the checkboxes below to show/hide the types of nodes and edges you want to visualize. 

The intra-cluster options are currently only available when **flat routing is enabled**. 

The **Highlight Fan-In Fan-Out Edges** option will highlight the fan-in and fan-out edges of the selected routing resource in blue and red, respectively. 

Multiple routing resources can be highlighted by pressing ctrl + mouse click.

.. figure:: ../Images/show_rr_graph.gif
    :align: center
    :width: 100%

    Visualizing Routing Architecture

**Node Colors**:

+------------+--------+
| Node Type  | Color  |
+============+========+
| Channel    | Black  |
+------------+--------+
| Input Pin  | Purple |
+------------+--------+
| Output Pin | Pink   |
+------------+--------+

**Edge Colors**:

+-----------------------+---------------+
| Edge Type             | Color         |
+=======================+===============+
| Pin to Output Pin     | Light Pink    |
+-----------------------+---------------+
| Pin to Input Pin      | Medium Purple |
+-----------------------+---------------+
| Output Pin to Channel | Pink          |
+-----------------------+---------------+
| Channel to Input Pin  | Purple        |
+-----------------------+---------------+
| Channel to Channel    | Dark Green    |
+-----------------------+---------------+
| Non-Configurable Edge | Dark Grey     |
+-----------------------+---------------+

The points at which wiring segments connect to clb pins (connection box switches) are marked with an ``x``.

Switch box connections will have buffers (triangles) or pass transistors (circles) drawn on top of them, depending on the type of switch each connection uses.

Routing Congestion
------------------------------
When a routing is shown on-screen, clicking on the **Congestion** drop-down menu under the **Routing Options** tab will show a heat map of any overused routing resources (wires or pins).
Lighter colours (e.g. yellow) correspond to highly overused resources, while darker colours (e.g. blue) correspond to lower overuse.
The overuse range shown at the bottom of the window.

.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/bitcoin_congestion.gif
    :align: center
    :width: 50%

    Routing Congestion during placement and routing

Routing Utilization
-------------------------------
When a routing is shown on-screen, clicking on the **Routing Util** drop-down menu will show a heat map of routing wire utilization (i.e. fraction of wires used in each channel).
Lighter colours (e.g. yellow) correspond to highly utilized channels, while darker colours (e.g. blue) correspond to lower utilization.

.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/bitcoin_routing_util.gif
    :align: center
    :width: 50%

    Routing Utilization during placement and routing

Block Internals
-------------------------------
During placement and routing you can adjust the level of block detail you visualize by using the **Block Internal** option under the **Block** tab. 

.. figure:: ../Images/Block_Settings.png
    :align: center 
    :width: 100%

    Block Settings 

Each block can contain a number of flip flops (ff), look up tables (lut), and other primitives. The higher the number, the deeper into the hierarchy within the cluster level block you see. 
The format of the internal block names is <block_type_name>[<placement_index>]:<mode_name>.

Layers Dropdown
-----------------------------
.. figure:: ../Images/view_menu.png
    :align: center 
    :width: 50%

    Items under the layers dropdown

The layers dropdown is displayed when vpr is targeting a stacked multi-die architecture (more than 1 layer).
Layers are drawn in ascending order for many drawing features (e.g. blocks); that is layer 0 is drawn first, and (if visible), layer 1 is drawn on top of it etc.
The visibility and transparency of a layer can be changed, which will affect blocks, nets, routing, and critical path.
Cross-layer connections refer to connections that are in different layers. 

Button Description Table
------------------------
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
|      Buttons      |      Stages       |        Functionalities       |     Detailed Descriptions    |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Blk Internal      | Placement/Routing | Controls depth of sub-blocks | Click multiple times to show |
|                   |                   | shown                        | more details; Click to reset |
|                   |                   |                              | when reached maximum level   |
|                   |                   |                              | of detail                    |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Toggle Block      | Placement/Routing | Adjusts the level of         | Click multiple times to      |
| Internal          |                   | visualized block detail      | go deeper into the           |
|                   |                   |                              | hierarchy within the cluster |
|                   |                   |                              | level block                  |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Blk Pin Util      | Placement/Routing | Visualizes block pin         | Click multiple times to      |
|                   |                   | utilization                  | visualize all block pin      |
|                   |                   |                              | utilization, input block pin |
|                   |                   |                              | utilization, or output block |
|                   |                   |                              | pin utilization              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Cong. Cost        | Routing           | Visualizes the congestion    |                              |
|                   |                   | costs of routing resources   |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Congestion        | Routing           | Visualizes a heat map of     |                              |
|                   |                   | overused routing resources   |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Crit. Path        | Placement/Routing | Visualizes the critical path |                              |
|                   |                   | of the circuit               |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Draw Partitions   | Placement/Routing | Visualizes placement         |                              |
|                   |                   | constraints                  |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Place Macros      | Placement/Routing | Visualizes placement macros  |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Route BB          | Routing           | Visualizes net bounding      | Click multiple times to      |
|                   |                   | boxes one by one             | sequence through the net     |
|                   |                   |                              | being shown                  |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Router Cost       | Routing           | Visualizes the router costs  |                              |
|                   |                   | of different routing         |                              |
|                   |                   | resources                    |                              |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Routing Util      | Routing           | Visualizes routing channel   |                              |
|                   |                   | utilization with colors      |                              |
|                   |                   | indicating the fraction of   |                              |
|                   |                   | wires used within a channel  |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Toggle Nets       | Placement/Routing | Visualizes the nets in the   | Click multiple times to      |
|                   |                   | circuit                      | set the nets to be visible / |
|                   |                   |                              | invisible                    |
|                   |                   |                              |                              |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+
| Toggle RR         | Placement/Routing | Visualizes different views   | Click multiple times to      |
|                   |                   | of the routing resources     | switch between routing       |
|                   |                   |                              | resources available in the   |
|                   |                   |                              | FPGA                         |
+-------------------+-------------------+------------------------------+------------------------------+

Manual Moves
------------

The manual moves feature allows the user to specify the next move in placement. If the move is legal, blocks are swapped and the new move is shown on the architecture. 

.. _fig-misc-tab:
.. figure:: ../Images/manual_move.png
    :align: center
    :width: 25%

    Misc. Tab

To enable the feature, activate the **Manual Move** toggle button under the **Misc.** tab and press Done. Alternatively, the user can activate the **Manual Move** toggle button and click on the block to be moved.

.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/draw_manual_moves_window.png
   :align: center
   :width: 50%

On the manual move window, the user can specify the Block ID/Block name of the block to move and the To location, with the x position, y position and subtile position. For the manual move to be valid:

- The To location requested by the user should be within the grid's dimensions.
- The block to be moved is found, valid and not fixed.
- The blocks to be swapped are compatible.
- The location chosen by the user is different from the block's current location.
  
If the manual move is legal, the cost summary window will display the delta cost, delta timing, delta bounding box cost and the placer's annealing decision that would result from this move. 

.. figure:: https://www.verilogtorouting.org/img/manual_move_cost_dialog.png
   :align: center
   :width: 50%

The user can Accept or Reject the manual move based on the values provided. If accepted the block's new location is shown. 

Pause Button
------------

The pause button allows the user to temporarily stop the program during placement or routing.
When clicked during the placement stage, the program will pause at the next temperature update.
When clicked during the routing stage, it will pause at the next router iteration.

The button can be pressed at any time while the program is running. To enable the feature, click the **Pause** button under the **Misc.** tab (see :ref:`fig-misc-tab`).
Once the program reaches the next temperature update or router iteration after the button is pressed, it will automatically pause.

After the program has paused, clicking **Next Step** allows the user to resume execution from the point where the program was paused.
This can be continuing from the current temperature in placement or from the current router iteration in routing.

75	ifeq ($(VERBOSE),1)
76	override CMAKE_PARAMS := -DVTR_ENABLE_VERBOSE=on ${CMAKE_PARAMS}
77	endif
78	
79	#`make ensure-gui` / `make ensure-headless` set VTR_GRAPHICS to force the
80	#graphics option. They delegate to the normal build rule below, so the PGO /
81	#build-dir / cmake handling is inherited rather than duplicated.
82	ifeq ($(VTR_GRAPHICS),on)
83	override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DVPR_USE_EZGL=on
84	endif
85	ifeq ($(VTR_GRAPHICS),off)
86	override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DVPR_USE_EZGL=off
87	endif
88	
89	# -s : Suppresses makefile output (e.g. entering/leaving directories)
90	# --output-sync target : For parallel compilation ensure output for each target is synchronized (make version >= 4.0)
91	MAKEFLAGS := -s
92	
93	ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
94	ifeq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
95	SOURCE_DIR := $(PWD)
96	else
97	SOURCE_DIR := $(shell powershell -NoProfile -Command "(Get-Location).Path")
98	SOURCE_DIR := $(subst \,/,$(SOURCE_DIR))
99	endif
100	else
101	SOURCE_DIR := $(PWD)
102	endif
103	BUILD_DIR ?= build
104	
105	#Check for the cmake executable
106	CMAKE := $(shell command -v cmake 2> /dev/null)
107	ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
108	ifneq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
109	CMAKE := cmake.exe
110	endif
111	endif
112	
113	#Show test log on failures with 'make test'
114	export CTEST_OUTPUT_ON_FAILURE=TRUE
115	
116	#All targets in this make file are always out of date.
117	# This ensures that any make target requests are forwarded to
118	# the generated makefile
119	.PHONY: all distclean ensure-gui ensure-headless $(MAKECMDGOALS)
120	
121	#For an 'all' build with BUILD_TYPE containing 'pgo' this will perform a 2-stage compilation
122	#with profile guided optimization.
123	#For a BUILD_TYPE without 'pgo', a single stage (non-pgo) compilation is performed.
124	
125	#Forward any targets that are not named 'distclean', 'clean', 'ensure-gui' or 'ensure-headless' to the generated Makefile
126	ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),distclean)
127	ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),clean)
128	ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),ensure-gui)
129	ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),ensure-headless)
130	all $(MAKECMDGOALS):
131	ifneq ($(BUILD_DIR),build)
132		ln -sf $(BUILD_DIR) build
133	endif
134	ifeq ($(CMAKE),)
135		ifeq ($(UNAME_S),Darwin)
136			$(error Required 'cmake' executable not found. On macOS try 'brew install cmake' to install)
137		else
138			$(error Required 'cmake' executable not found. On debian/ubuntu try 'sudo apt-get install cmake' to install)
139		endif
140	endif
141		@ mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
142	ifneq (,$(findstring pgo,$(BUILD_TYPE)))
143		#
144		#Profile Guided Optimization Build
145		#
146		@echo "Performing Profile Guided Optimization (PGO) build..."
147		#
148		#1st-stage build for profile generation
149		#
150		echo "cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_gen $(SOURCE_DIR)"
151		cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_gen $(SOURCE_DIR)
152		@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) $(MAKECMDGOALS)
153		#
154		#Run benchmarks to generate profiling data
155		#
156		echo "Generating profile data for PGO (may take several minutes)"
157		#Need titan benchmarks for pgo_profile task
158		@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) get_titan_benchmarks
159		#Note profiling must be done serially to avoid corrupting the generated profiles
160		./run_reg_test.py pgo_profile
161		#
162		#Configure 2nd-stage build to use profiling data to guide compiler optimization
163		#
164		echo "cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_use $(SOURCE_DIR)"
165		cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_use $(SOURCE_DIR)
166	else #BUILD_TYPE not containing 'pgo'
167		#
168		#Configure for standard build
169		#
170		@echo "Performing standard build..."
171		echo "cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) $(SOURCE_DIR)"
172		cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) $(SOURCE_DIR)
173	endif #BUILD_TYPE
174		#
175		#Final build
176		#
177	ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
178	# MSYS2 is based on Makefile
179	ifeq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
180		@echo "Building target(s): $(MAKECMDGOALS)"
181		@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) $(MAKECMDGOALS)
182	else
183	# MSVC is based on Ninja, use cmake native build
184		@echo "Building target(s): $(MAKECMDGOALS)"
185		$(CMAKE) --build $(BUILD_DIR)
186	endif # final build in windows
187	else
188	# Linux build is based on Makefile
189		@echo "Building target(s): $(MAKECMDGOALS)"
190		@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) $(MAKECMDGOALS)
191	endif # final build
192	endif #ensure-headless
193	endif #ensure-gui
194	endif #clean
195	endif #distclean
196	
197	#Build VTR WITH graphics. Provision a Qt6 SDK (>= the supported floor) first,
198	#honoring ensure_qt6_sdk.sh's exit code (make aborts if it fails), then delegate
199	#to the normal build with graphics forced on. Defined after the 'all' rule so
200	#'all' stays the default goal.
201	#Builds the default 'all' goal (not just 'vpr') so the full flow -- synthesis
202	#(yosys + parmys), mapping (abc) and place-and-route (vpr) -- is produced. The
203	#yosys/parmys targets are 'ALL' custom targets that 'make vpr' would skip,
204	#leaving build/bin/yosys missing and run_vtr_flow unable to find yosys.
205	ensure-gui:
206		@ $(SOURCE_DIR)/dev/ensure_qt6_sdk.sh
207		@+$(MAKE) all VTR_GRAPHICS=on
208	
209	#Build VTR WITHOUT graphics (headless); no Qt SDK is needed.
210	ensure-headless:
211		@+$(MAKE) all VTR_GRAPHICS=off
212	
213	#Call the generated Makefile's clean, and then remove all cmake generated files
214	distclean: clean

file.content:
ifeq ($(VERBOSE),1)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := -DVTR_ENABLE_VERBOSE=on ${CMAKE_PARAMS}
endif

#`make ensure-gui` / `make ensure-headless` set VTR_GRAPHICS to force the
#graphics option. They delegate to the normal build rule below, so the PGO /
#build-dir / cmake handling is inherited rather than duplicated.
ifeq ($(VTR_GRAPHICS),on)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DVPR_USE_EZGL=on
endif
ifeq ($(VTR_GRAPHICS),off)
override CMAKE_PARAMS := ${CMAKE_PARAMS} -DVPR_USE_EZGL=off
endif

# -s : Suppresses makefile output (e.g. entering/leaving directories)
# --output-sync target : For parallel compilation ensure output for each target is synchronized (make version >= 4.0)
MAKEFLAGS := -s

ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
ifeq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
SOURCE_DIR := $(PWD)
else
SOURCE_DIR := $(shell powershell -NoProfile -Command "(Get-Location).Path")
SOURCE_DIR := $(subst \,/,$(SOURCE_DIR))
endif
else
SOURCE_DIR := $(PWD)
endif
BUILD_DIR ?= build

#Check for the cmake executable
CMAKE := $(shell command -v cmake 2> /dev/null)
ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
ifneq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
CMAKE := cmake.exe
endif
endif

#Show test log on failures with 'make test'
export CTEST_OUTPUT_ON_FAILURE=TRUE

#All targets in this make file are always out of date.
# This ensures that any make target requests are forwarded to
# the generated makefile
.PHONY: all distclean ensure-gui ensure-headless $(MAKECMDGOALS)

#For an 'all' build with BUILD_TYPE containing 'pgo' this will perform a 2-stage compilation
#with profile guided optimization.
#For a BUILD_TYPE without 'pgo', a single stage (non-pgo) compilation is performed.

#Forward any targets that are not named 'distclean', 'clean', 'ensure-gui' or 'ensure-headless' to the generated Makefile
ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),distclean)
ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),clean)
ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),ensure-gui)
ifneq ($(MAKECMDGOALS),ensure-headless)
all $(MAKECMDGOALS):
ifneq ($(BUILD_DIR),build)
	ln -sf $(BUILD_DIR) build
endif
ifeq ($(CMAKE),)
	ifeq ($(UNAME_S),Darwin)
		$(error Required 'cmake' executable not found. On macOS try 'brew install cmake' to install)
	else
		$(error Required 'cmake' executable not found. On debian/ubuntu try 'sudo apt-get install cmake' to install)
	endif
endif
	@ mkdir -p $(BUILD_DIR)
ifneq (,$(findstring pgo,$(BUILD_TYPE)))
	#
	#Profile Guided Optimization Build
	#
	@echo "Performing Profile Guided Optimization (PGO) build..."
	#
	#1st-stage build for profile generation
	#
	echo "cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_gen $(SOURCE_DIR)"
	cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_gen $(SOURCE_DIR)
	@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) $(MAKECMDGOALS)
	#
	#Run benchmarks to generate profiling data
	#
	echo "Generating profile data for PGO (may take several minutes)"
	#Need titan benchmarks for pgo_profile task
	@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) get_titan_benchmarks
	#Note profiling must be done serially to avoid corrupting the generated profiles
	./run_reg_test.py pgo_profile
	#
	#Configure 2nd-stage build to use profiling data to guide compiler optimization
	#
	echo "cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_use $(SOURCE_DIR)"
	cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) -DVPR_PGO_CONFIG=prof_use $(SOURCE_DIR)
else #BUILD_TYPE not containing 'pgo'
	#
	#Configure for standard build
	#
	@echo "Performing standard build..."
	echo "cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) $(SOURCE_DIR)"
	cd $(BUILD_DIR) && $(CMAKE) $(CMAKE_PARAMS) $(SOURCE_DIR)
endif #BUILD_TYPE
	#
	#Final build
	#
ifeq ($(OS),Windows_NT)
# MSYS2 is based on Makefile
ifeq ($(MSYSTEM),MINGW64)
	@echo "Building target(s): $(MAKECMDGOALS)"
	@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) $(MAKECMDGOALS)
else
# MSVC is based on Ninja, use cmake native build
	@echo "Building target(s): $(MAKECMDGOALS)"
	$(CMAKE) --build $(BUILD_DIR)
endif # final build in windows
else
# Linux build is based on Makefile
	@echo "Building target(s): $(MAKECMDGOALS)"
	@+$(MAKE) -C $(BUILD_DIR) $(MAKECMDGOALS)
endif # final build
endif #ensure-headless
endif #ensure-gui
endif #clean
endif #distclean

#Build VTR WITH graphics. Provision a Qt6 SDK (>= the supported floor) first,
#honoring ensure_qt6_sdk.sh's exit code (make aborts if it fails), then delegate
#to the normal build with graphics forced on. Defined after the 'all' rule so
#'all' stays the default goal.
#Builds the default 'all' goal (not just 'vpr') so the full flow -- synthesis
#(yosys + parmys), mapping (abc) and place-and-route (vpr) -- is produced. The
#yosys/parmys targets are 'ALL' custom targets that 'make vpr' would skip,
#leaving build/bin/yosys missing and run_vtr_flow unable to find yosys.
ensure-gui:
	@ $(SOURCE_DIR)/dev/ensure_qt6_sdk.sh
	@+$(MAKE) all VTR_GRAPHICS=on

#Build VTR WITHOUT graphics (headless); no Qt SDK is needed.
ensure-headless:
	@+$(MAKE) all VTR_GRAPHICS=off

#Call the generated Makefile's clean, and then remove all cmake generated files
distclean: clean

"specifying the --analysis option.");

    auto& gfx_grp = parser.add_argument_group("graphics options");

    gfx_grp.add_argument<bool, ParseOnOff>(args.show_graphics, "--disp")
        .help(
            "Enable or disable interactive graphics."
            " When 'off' and QT_QPA_PLATFORM is not already set, VPR sets"
            " QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen so Qt does not try to connect to"
            " an X11/Wayland display. Offscreen means no interactive window"
            " is opened, but graphics are still rendered, so scripted output"
            " (e.g. --save_graphics and --graphics_commands) still works."
            " Note that the offscreen platform typically disables the RHI"
            " (GPU) renderer; rendering falls back to the QPainter (immediate)"
            " path. To override, set QT_QPA_PLATFORM in the environment before"
            " invoking VPR.")
        .default_value("off");

    gfx_grp.add_argument(args.GraphPause, "--auto")
        .help(
            "Controls how often VPR pauses for interactive"
            " graphics (requiring Proceed to be clicked)."
            " Higher values pause less frequently")
        .default_value("1")
        .choices({"0", "1", "2"})
        .show_in(argparse::ShowIn::HELP_ONLY);

    gfx_grp.add_argument<bool, ParseOnOff>(args.save_graphics, "--save_graphics")
        .help("Save all graphical contents to PDF files")
        .default_value("off");

    gfx_grp.add_argument(args.graphics_renderer, "--renderer")
        .help(
            "Select the rendering backend used by the graphics window.\n"
            "   * rhi:       GPU hardware rendering. Gives the highest\n"
            "                performance but requires a GPU (uses VRAM),\n"
            "                and uses the most RAM.\n"
            "   * deferred:  SW renderer (no GPU). Like 'immediate' but\n"
            "                batches draw calls, giving the next highest\n"
            "                performance at the cost of more RAM.\n"
            "   * immediate: SW renderer (no GPU). The most compatible path\n"
            "                (CPU-only QPainter, no batching); lowest\n"
            "                performance and lowest memory use.")
        .default_value("rhi")
        .choices({"immediate", "deferred", "rhi"})
        .show_in(argparse::ShowIn::HELP_ONLY);

    gfx_grp.add_argument(args.graphics_commands, "--graphics_commands")
        .help(
            "A set of semi-colon separated graphics commands. \n"
            "Commands must be surrounded by quotation marks (e.g. --graphics_commands \"save_graphics place.png\")\n"
            "   Commands:\n"
            "      * save_graphics <file>\n"
            "           Saves graphics to the specified file (.png/.pdf/\n"

            "           scale is needed across images\n"
            "      * set_draw_block_outlines <int>\n"
            "           Sets whether blocks have an outline drawn around\n"
            "           them\n"
            "      * set_draw_block_text <int>\n"
            "           Sets whether blocks have label text drawn on them\n"

content.text: 让我先阅读一下最近的 u8 讲义（u8-l3），以确保其风格和标题规范与我将要编写的文件保持一致。

content.content:
1	# 功耗分析
2	
3	## 1. 本讲目标
4	
5	在前几讲里，我们已经能让 VPR 把一个电路**布通**（u6 布线）并报出**关键路径时序**（u7 时序分析）。但「速度」与「面积」之外，FPGA 还有一个同样重要的评估维度：**功耗（Power）**。本讲回答三个问题：
6	
7	1. VPR 是在流程的哪个时刻、用什么入口去估算整块 FPGA 的功耗的？（**功耗估算总流程**）
8	2. 这个总功耗是怎么**分解**成布线 / 时钟 / 逻辑块三大块，再进一步拆成动态（dynamic）与漏电（leakage）的？（**组件功耗分解**）
9	3. 估算动态功耗必须知道「每根线翻转多频繁」，估算漏电必须知道「晶体管特性」——这两类 VPR 自己算不出来的外部数据从哪来？（**活动性与工艺数据**）
10	
11	学完本讲，你应当能：看懂 VPR 的 `--power` 开关如何触发一次独立的分析流程；读懂 `.power` 报告里三层 breakdown 的含义；说清楚 ACE2、活动性文件 `.act` 与 CMOS 工艺 XML 三者在功耗流程里的分工。
12	
13	## 2. 前置知识
14	
15	本讲默认你已经掌握 u7（时序分析）的核心结论：**布线之后**才能做静态时序分析，时序分析会产出一条关键路径及其延迟 `T_crit`。功耗分析与时序分析是「同一批收尾工作」的两个分支——它们都依赖已经完成的布局布线结果。此外请回忆两个贯穿全书的概念：
16	
17	- **架构驱动**：目标 FPGA 的物理结构来自架构 XML，VPR 算法绝不硬编码架构假设。功耗同样如此：晶体管尺寸、线电容、PN 比例这些「物理量」都来自外部 XML，VPR 只负责「在给定物理量下算账」。
18	- **全局状态总线 `g_vpr_ctx`**：每个阶段把自己的产物写进对应的子上下文。功耗阶段读写的是 [`PowerContext`](#)，它和 AtomContext、RoutingContext、PlacementContext 并列。
19	
20	下面补充三个本讲会用到的、但前序讲义没细讲的物理常识：
21	
22	- **动态功耗（dynamic power）**：电容每次充放电都消耗能量，和「信号翻转有多频繁」成正比。一次 0→1→0 的翻转给负载电容 $C$ 充了又放了 $C V_{dd}$ 的电荷，消耗能量 $\tfrac{1}{2} C V_{dd}^2$。所以动态功耗既依赖工艺（$C$、$V_{dd}$），又依赖**电路行为**（翻转频率）。后者正是 VPR 算不出来的部分。
23	- **漏电功耗 / 静态功耗（leakage power）**：晶体管关断时仍有的亚阈值漏电流，只要上电就一直存在，和翻转无关，只依赖工艺与器件尺寸。
24	- **信号活动性（activity）**：用两个量刻画一根线长期的行为——**静态概率** $P$（长期来看取值为 1 的概率）与**翻转密度** $D$（平均每个时钟周期翻转几次，时钟本身 $D=2$）。两者都需要在综合后通过**仿真/概率传播**估计，VPR 不做这件事，交给 ACE2。
25

42	## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解
43	
44	本讲按三个最小模块组织：先讲清「总流程」是怎么被触发、怎么走完一遍；再讲它内部如何「分解」；最后讲它依赖的两类「外部数据」从哪来。
45	
46	---
47	
48	### 4.1 功耗估算总流程
49	
50	#### 4.1.1 概念说明
51	
52	功耗估算在 VPR 中是一个**可选的、布线后的独立分析步骤**。它不是布局布线算法的一部分（不会反过来影响布局布线决策），而是布线完成后「看一眼这块已经布好的 FPGA 大概耗多少电」。因此它：
53	
54	- **必须是后置的**：需要已经完成的布局（每块在哪）、布线（每根网走哪些导线、驱动哪些 mux）、时序（关键路径周期 `T_crit` 决定时钟频率）。
55	- **必须是可选的**：默认关闭，由 `--power` 开关开启；而且开启它必须额外喂两个外部文件（活动性 + CMOS 工艺），缺一不可。
56	- **有自己的生命周期**：和时序分析一样，走一条 `init → 跑 → uninit` 的三步路，用完即释放内存。
57	
58	#### 4.1.2 核心流程
59	
60	整个功耗估算在 VPR 内部的调用链如下（从触发到结束）：
61	
62	```
63	vpr_flow()
64	  └─ vpr_analysis_flow()              # 布线后的分析阶段（含时序报告）
65	       └─ if (PowerOpts.do_power):    # 由 --power 开关控制
66	            vpr_power_estimation(...)  # 本讲的唯一入口
67	              ├─ 1. 取 T_crit（关键路径延迟）与 channel_width 写入 solution_inf
68	              ├─ 2. power_init(PowerFile, CmosTechFile, Arch, RoutingArch)
69	              │      ├─ power_tech_init(CmosTechFile)   # 读 CMOS 工艺 XML
70	              │      ├─ power_components_init()          # 准备组件跟踪表
71	              │      ├─ power_calibrate()                # 用 SPICE 校准数据标定
72	              │      └─ power_sizing_init()              # 为各组件定尺寸
73	              ├─ 3. power_total(...)                     # 真正算：布线+时钟+逻辑块
74	              └─ 4. power_uninit()                       # 释放
75	```
76	
77	注意一个**时机细节**：活动性文件（`.act`）的读入**不在** `vpr_power_estimation` 里，而在更早的初始化阶段 `vpr_init_with_options` 里就完成了——因为读活动性需要已经建好的原子网表 `AtomNetlist`，而那是在读 BLIF 之后立刻就有的。
78	
79	#### 4.1.3 源码精读
80	
81	**入口声明**——功耗模块对外只暴露三个函数，定义在 [`power.h:L304-L312`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/power/power.h#L304-L312)。`power_init` 接收功耗输出文件路径、CMOS 工艺文件路径、架构指针与路由架构；`power_total` 是真正算总功耗的函数；`power_uninit` 负责释放。
82	
83	**触发点**——在主流程的 `vpr_analysis_flow` 末尾，由 `PowerOpts.do_power` 守卫，见 [`vpr_api.cpp:L1641-L1643`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L1641-L1643)。这说明功耗与时序报告是同一批「收尾分析」的两个并列分支。
84	
85	**入口实现 `vpr_power_estimation`**——见 [`vpr_api.cpp:L1656-L1716`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L1656-L1716)。它先把布线/时序结果里的两个关键标量写进 `PowerContext.solution_inf`：
86	
87	- [`vpr_api.cpp:L1668`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L1668)：`T_crit = timing_info.least_slack_critical_path().delay()`——关键路径延迟，决定时钟频率 $f = 1/T_{crit}$，进而决定动态功耗里的 $f$ 项。
88	- [`vpr_api.cpp:L1672`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L1672)：`channel_width = route_status.chan_width()`——最终布线用的通道宽度，影响布线功耗规模。
89	
90	接着 [`vpr_api.cpp:L1681-L1682`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L1681-L1682) 调用 `power_init`，两个文件实参正是来自 `FileNameOpts.PowerFile`（功耗报告输出路径）和 `FileNameOpts.CmosTechFile`（CMOS 工艺文件）。随后 [`vpr_api.cpp:L1693`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L1693) 调用 `power_total` 跑估算。注意第 1661 行还有一个**前置约束**：功耗分析只支持单时钟电路，多时钟直接 `VPR_FATAL_ERROR`。
91	
92	**活动性文件读入时机**——见 [`vpr_api.cpp:L333-L338`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_api.cpp#L333-L338)：读 BLIF 建好 `AtomNetlist` 之后，若 `do_power` 为真，立刻 `read_activity` 把 `.act` 解析进 `PowerContext.atom_net_power`。这发生在所有阶段开始之前，可见活动性数据是「预先备好」的全局输入。
93	
94	**`power_total` 主算函数**——见 [`power.cpp:L1705-L1772`](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/power/power.cpp#L1705-L1772)。它依次算三大组件、各自累加进 `total_power`，最后写报告。第 1717 行还拒绝双向布线架构（`BI_DIRECTIONAL`），是模型适用性的硬约束。
95

97	
98	**实践目标**：验证「功耗估算所需输入」的来源，把第 4.1.2 节的调用链落到具体代码行。

106	
107	**需要观察的现象**：`vpr_power_estimation` 自身只显式读了 `PowerFile` 与 `CmosTechFile` 两个文件；活动性文件 `ActFile` 在更早的初始化阶段就被消费了。
108	
109	**预期结果**：能画出一张「输入来源表」：
110	
111	| 输入 | 命令行选项 | 在哪里读入 | 来源 |
112	|------|-----------|-----------|------|
113	| 活动性 `.act` | `--activity_file` | `vpr_init_with_options`（`vpr_api.cpp:337`） | ACE2 估算 |
114	| CMOS 工艺 XML | `--tech_properties` | `power_init`（`vpr_api.cpp:1681`） | PTM 工艺模型 |
115	| 功耗报告 `.power` | （输出） | `power_init` 内 `fopen` | VPR 自己写 |
116	| `T_crit` | （非文件） | `vpr_power_estimation:1668` | 时序分析 |
